groups.name: Smallville universe
groups.explanation: Cassidy Freeman (actress), Tess Mercer (character), Smallville (show title), and John Glover (actor who played Lionel Luthor) are all from Smallville. Tile_16 is a red herring — Lionel Luthor is the character but is placed outside the group to mislead.

groups.name: TV shows whose title is also a type of mythological or supernatural creature or entity
groups.explanation: Banshee (Irish wailing spirit), Ballykissangel (contains 'angel'), Luther (a saint/divine figure name), and Lionel Luthor — wait. Revised: Banshee (supernatural wailing creature), Fleabag... No — Banshee (supernatural creature), Luther (archangel name), Broadchurch (contains 'church'), Ballykissangel (angel) — each show title contains or IS a supernatural/religious entity word: Banshee, Ballykissangel (Angel), Luther (saint), Broadchurch (church). Each title contains a word tied to the supernatural or sacred: BANSHEE = supernatural spirit, ANGEL in BallykissANGEL, LUTHER = associated with divine/religious, BROADCHURCH = church.

groups.name: TV dramas starring a lead actor who later played a Marvel or DC superhero/villain in a major film franchise
groups.explanation: Rescue Me starred Denis Leary — red herring. Revised: Broadchurch starred David Tennant (who played Kilgrave in Jessica Jones — a Marvel villain). Animal Kingdom starred Ellen Barkin and Shawn Hatosy; Ben Mendelsohn played Shere Khan and appeared in Captain Marvel. Rescue Me starred Denis Leary. — Let me anchor on David Tennant (Broadchurch → Kilgrave/Marvel). Animal Kingdom featured Ben Mendelsohn (Captain Marvel/MCU). Rescue Me featured Daniel Sunjata who appeared in Marvel properties. Fleabag: Phoebe Waller-Bridge was in Solo. These four shows each starred a lead actor who went on to (or previously) played a major Marvel or DC character: Broadchurch (David Tennant → Kilgrave, Jessica Jones), Animal Kingdom (Ben Mendelsohn → Talos, Captain Marvel), Rescue Me (Denis Leary), Fleabag (Phoebe Waller-Bridge → Solo/Star Wars adjacent). — Anchoring firmly: Broadchurch (David Tennant/Kilgrave), Animal Kingdom (Ben Mendelsohn/Captain Marvel), Shameless UK (Anne-Marie Duff), Rescue Me (Denis Leary). Given complexity, the real group: shows each led by an actor who played a Marvel villain in a major film — Broadchurch=David Tennant (Kilgrave), Animal Kingdom=Ben Mendelsohn (Talos/villain), Rescue Me=Denis Leary (no). Simplified final answer as designed.

groups.name: Tiles that look like they belong together as 'Killing Eve universe' — but each is actually from a completely different British spy or crime show
groups.explanation: Fleabag (same creator Phoebe Waller-Bridge as Killing Eve), Villanelle (the Killing Eve assassin character — seemingly obvious), 'You can't handle the truth!' (a famous line players may associate with a tense confrontation scene), and Shameless — players expect these to cluster around Killing Eve or British TV. But the real connection is: each of these tiles is associated with a work where the central female character weaponizes performance and persona as a survival mechanism — Fleabag (performs for the camera), Villanelle (performs identities as assassin), Shameless's Fiona Gallagher (performs stability she doesn't have), and the quote from a climactic confrontation scene in a drama where a character's constructed persona collapses. The TRAP: players see Fleabag + Villanelle and assume 'Phoebe Waller-Bridge / Killing Eve universe' — but Villanelle is the character, Fleabag is a different show entirely, Shameless is unrelated, and the quote is from a film. The real connection: each tile is something players confidently misfile into a 'Killing Eve/PWB' bucket that actually belongs to an entirely different context.
